rationale: The sole ground-truth substantive concern (yJader's point that already_submitted shouldn't gate the unwrap because both submit_initial and submit_update reject list prompts) is nailed precisely by Y, which cites exact stage_pool.py line numbers (951-955, 1022-1026) proving both paths raise ValueError on lists — an almost verbatim match to the reviewer's reasoning. X captures the same conclusion more loosely ('confirmed correct across all four state combinations') without the same grounded evidence tying the two submission paths together. X also pads its review with speculative, diff-external claims (an issue #4839/HunyuanImage-3.0 tie-in citing a specific commenter) that can't be verified against the given diff, hurting precision, and lands on a COMMENT verdict where both actual reviewers approved, while Y correctly recommends APPROVE. Both are reasonably actionable with file/line citations and concrete test suggestions, but Y's findings are tighter, better grounded, and more aligned with what actually mattered in this PR.
